verses.commentary: I am the true vine, and My Father is the gardener. Seventh 'I AM' statement. True (alēthinos, ἀληθινός): genuine, real. Israel was false vine (Isaiah 5, Jeremiah 2:21). Jesus is authentic. Father as vinedresser. Cultivation, pruning.

He cuts off every branch in Me that bears no fruit, while every branch that does bear fruit He prunes so that it will be even more fruitful. Two kinds of branches. Fruitless: removed. Fruitful: pruned. Kathairō (καθαίρω), prune, cleanse. Discipline for productivity. Suffering produces fruit.
You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you. Katharos (καθαρός), clean. Word link to pruning (kathairō). His word cleanses. Already accomplished. Chapter 13:10 connection.

Remain in Me, as I also remain in you. No branch can bear fruit by itself; it must remain in the vine. Neither can you bear fruit unless you remain in Me. Menō (μένω), abide, remain. Mutual abiding. Organic connection essential. Separation means fruitlessness. Dependency absolute.

I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in Me and I in you, you will bear much fruit; apart from me you can do nothing. Identity clear. Vine/branch distinction. Condition: abiding. Result: much fruit. Apart from Me: chōris emou (χωρὶς ἐμοῦ). Nothing: ouden (οὐδέν), absolutely nothing. Total dependency.

If you do not remain in Me, you are like a branch that is thrown away and withers; such branches are picked up, thrown into the fire and burned. Consequence of non-abiding. Withering. Burning. Judgment language. Fruitless branches discarded. Eternal stakes.

If you remain in Me and My words remain in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you. Prayer promise. Condition: abiding and word-indwelling. Aligned desires. Answered prayer for abiding disciples.

This is to My Father's glory, that you bear much fruit, showing yourselves to be My disciples. Purpose: Father's glory. Fruit validates discipleship. Much fruit: polys karpos (πολὺς καρπός). Evidence of authentic faith.
As the Father has loved Me, so have I loved you. Now remain in My love. Love chain. Father to Son. Son to disciples. Command: remain in love. Abiding in love, not just experiencing it.

If you keep My commands, you will remain in My love, just as I have kept My Father's commands and remain in His love. Condition for abiding. Obedience maintains love relationship. Jesus models. His obedience to Father parallels disciples' obedience to Him.
I have told you this so that My joy may be in you and that your joy may be complete. Purpose: joy. His joy in them. Their joy complete. Peplērōmenē (πεπληρωμένη), filled full. Obedience produces joy.
My command is this: Love each other as I have loved you. Singular command. Agapaō (ἀγαπάω), sacrificial love. Standard: Jesus' love for them. Cross-shaped love.

Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one's life for one's friends. Ultimate love defined. Self-sacrifice. Tithēmi psychēn (τίθημι ψυχήν), lay down life. Jesus will demonstrate. Disciples called to imitate.
You are My friends if you do what I command. Friendship conditional. Obedience defines relationship. Not servile but filial obedience.

I no longer call you servants, because a servant does not know his master's business. Instead, I have called you friends, for everything that I learned from My Father I have made known to you. Status upgrade. Doulos (δοῦλος), slave, to philos (φίλος), friend. Knowledge shared. Father's revelation transmitted. Intimacy, not hierarchy.

You did not choose Me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you might go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, and so that whatever you ask in My name the Father will give you. Divine initiative. They did not elect Him; He elected them. Purpose: fruitful mission. Lasting fruit. Prayer promise reiterated.
This is My command: Love each other. Repetition. Love as central imperative. Community love essential.
If the world hates you, keep in mind that it hated Me first. Hatred warning. World's animosity. Jesus experienced it first. Disciples will follow. Priority of hatred.

If you belonged to the world, it would love you as its own. As it is, you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world. That is why the world hates you. Explanation for hatred. Non-belonging. Ek tou kosmou (ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου), out of the world. Chosen out. Separation produces hostility.

Remember what I told you: A servant is not greater than his master. If they persecuted Me, they will persecute you also. If they obeyed My teaching, they will obey yours also. Restatement from 13:16. Servant follows master's treatment. Persecution guaranteed. Minority will obey.
They will treat you this way because of My name, for they do not know the One who sent Me. Cause of persecution. Jesus' name. Ignorance of Father. Hatred rooted in not knowing God.

If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin. Revelation brings responsibility. Ignorance would limit guilt. Knowledge removes excuse. Light increases accountability.
Whoever hates Me hates My Father as well. Inseparable unity. Cannot hate Jesus and love Father. Rejection of Son is rejection of Father.

If I had not done among them the works no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin. As it is, they have seen, and yet they have hated both Me and My Father. Works bring accountability. Unprecedented miracles. Evidence abundant. Seeing and hating. Willful rejection despite proof.
But this is to fulfill what is written in their Law: They hated Me without reason. Psalm 35:19, 69:4. Irrational hatred prophesied. Their Law: ironic. Scripture testifies against them.

When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who goes out from the Father, He will testify about Me. Spirit's witness. Sent from Father by Jesus. Procession. Spirit's mission: testify about Jesus. Christocentric witness.

And you also must testify, for you have been with Me from the beginning. Disciples' witness. Eyewitness testimony. Beginning (archē, ἀρχή): start of ministry. Apostolic foundation. Human and divine witness together.
